ip_outcome_note: Card is labeled 'Forward / Retainer' with category 'Final Fantasy XV' on the card itself. Depicted figure matches Ignis Scientia: glasses, swept-back light hair, fitted dark suit, composed posture. Character is a royal retainer (House Scientia) and childhood advisor to Prince Noctis in FFXV. EX Burst ability searches for Noctis card — mechanically encoding the retainer-to-prince relationship. IP confirmed via card oracle text (category: XV, job: Retainer), FFXV character design record (Wikipedia: Ignis Scientia), and character name on the card itself. Confidence: high.

trivia_bullets.text: Ignis Scientia was designed by Tetsuya Nomura and has been characterized as a composed, analytical counterpart to Noctis's impulsive nature — the English localization team modeled his characterization on 'a younger James Bond or Sherlock Holmes.' He entered Noctis's service at age six as heir to the royal-retainer House Scientia. [Wikipedia: Ignis Scientia; WebSearch snippet citing Fandom FF Wiki]
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki (Fandom) — Ignis Scientia, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: The 'EX' suffix in the card name 'Ignis EX' is not part of the character's name — it denotes the EX Burst mechanic: when this card is placed into the Damage Zone (when the opponent's attack resolves), its enters-the-field Auto ability triggers, letting the controlling player search their deck for a card named Noctis. This mechanically encodes the retainer-to-prince relationship directly in the damage-step trigger. [Final Fantasy TCG Wiki — EX Burst mechanics, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Final Fantasy Trading Card Game Wiki — EX Burst, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Beyond the EX Burst, Ignis (8-072R) also has an Overwhelm ability: pay one Earth + one generic, choose 1 Forward, and deal it 4000 damage for each Category XV Forward you control — scaling damage based on the party size, reflecting Ignis's role as the tactical brain whose effectiveness grows with the full Brotherhood assembled. [Game-O-Storus; WebSearch snippet via TCGPlayer product listing]

Opus VIII (March 2019) introduced Final Fantasy XV characters into the FFTCG for the first time as a standalone title set, alongside FFVII, FFIX, and FFXIV: Stormblood. Ignis appears as one of the Brotherhood members in the set; Noctis also appears separately. [TradingCardSets.com — FFTCG Opus VIII Complete Guide; Square Enix FFTCG release page]
